findings.title: Path Traversal Vulnerability in SessionStart_handoff_restore.py
findings.description: The _safe_id() function uses allow-list approach but doesn't prevent path traversal sequences like '../' in file paths. While it filters glob metacharacters, it doesn't validate against path traversal attacks when constructing paths from user-controlled task names or terminal IDs.
findings.impact: An attacker could craft task names with '../' sequences to write files outside intended directory, potentially overwriting arbitrary files.
findings.remediation: Use pathlib.Path.resolve() with strict=True to validate paths are within expected directory, or whitelist specific directories and enforce base directory constraints.

findings.title: Insufficient Input Validation in HandoffStore
findings.description: HandoffStore.__init__() accepts project_root and terminal_id without validation. Malicious terminal_id values could cause file operations outside intended directories.
findings.impact: File system operations could target unintended directories, causing data leakage or corruption.
findings.remediation: Validate terminal_id format (e.g., regex ^term_[a-zA-Z0-9_-]+$) and ensure project_root exists and is a directory.

findings.title: Race Condition in File Locking
findings.description: The file locking implementation has a TOCTOU (time-of-check-time-of-use) vulnerability. Between checking for lock file existence and creating it, another process could create the lock, leading to concurrent writes.
findings.impact: Concurrent compaction operations could corrupt task tracker files or cause data loss.
findings.remediation: Use platform-specific file locking (fcntl.flock on Unix, msvcrt.locking on Windows) or atomic operations.

findings.title: Checksum Comparison Timing Attack
findings.description: Checksum comparison using startswith() is vulnerable to timing attacks. An attacker could measure response times to brute-force valid checksum prefixes.
findings.impact: Remote attacker could potentially forge valid checksums for corrupted handoff data.
findings.remediation: Use hmac.compare_digest() for constant-time comparison of checksums.

findings.title: Inefficient File Search in SessionStart
findings.description: The slow path searches all terminal task files by globbing and reading each JSON file. For projects with many terminals, this could cause significant startup delay.
findings.impact: Session startup could take several seconds scanning hundreds of terminal files.
findings.remediation: Maintain a manifest file indexing active_session/continue_session tasks, or use database for O(1) lookup.

findings.title: Repeated JSON Serialization in Quality Scoring
findings.description: Quality scoring involves JSON serialization just to calculate size, then the data is serialized again when writing. This is duplicate work.
findings.impact: Unnecessary CPU overhead during compaction, especially for large handoffs.
findings.remediation: Cache serialized JSON or calculate size incrementally during construction.

findings.title: No Pagination for Large Lists
findings.description: When displaying modifications or active files, all items are loaded into memory before slicing. For very large lists, this wastes memory.
findings.impact: High memory usage for handoffs with thousands of modifications.
findings.remediation: Use itertools.islice() or database pagination for lazy loading.

findings.title: Inconsistent Error Handling
findings.description: atomic_write_with_validation() catches OSError but not other exceptions that could occur during file operations (e.g., PermissionError on Windows).
findings.impact: Unhandled exceptions could crash the compaction process, causing data loss.
findings.remediation: Catch Exception broadly and log specific types, or explicitly handle all relevant exception types.

findings.title: Magic Numbers Without Constants
findings.description: Size limits are defined as constants, but some magic numbers remain (e.g., retry count 5, lock wait 5 seconds). These are scattered throughout the code.
findings.impact: Difficult to maintain consistent behavior across the codebase.
findings.remediation: Define all configuration values in a central config module with clear documentation.

findings.title: Missing Type Hints in Public APIs
findings.description: Helper functions like extract_topic_from_content() lack complete type hints (e.g., return type could be more specific).
findings.evidence: def extract_topic_from_content(content: str, task_name: str = "") -> str:
findings.impact: Reduced IDE support and type safety for consumers of these APIs.
findings.remediation: Add comprehensive type hints to all public APIs, use TypedDict where appropriate.

findings.title: Missing Security Tests for Path Traversal
findings.description: No tests found for path traversal attacks. The _safe_id() function and file path construction are not tested against malicious inputs like '../../../etc/passwd'.
findings.evidence: Test files exist but none test path traversal scenarios.
findings.impact: Security vulnerabilities could go undetected in production.
findings.remediation: Add test suite for input validation covering path traversal, null bytes, and unicode attacks.

findings.title: No Concurrency Tests
findings.description: No tests for concurrent compaction scenarios. File locking and race conditions are not tested with multiple processes/threads.
findings.evidence: All tests appear to be single-threaded.
findings.impact: Race conditions could corrupt data in production with concurrent sessions.
findings.remediation: Add integration tests using multiprocessing to simulate concurrent compaction.

findings.title: Missing Edge Case Tests for Checksum Validation
findings.description: Tests for checksum validation don't cover edge cases like empty checksums, malformed checksums (wrong length, invalid hex), or checksums with different cases.
findings.evidence: test_checkpoint_validation_error_paths.py exists but may not cover all checksum edge cases.
findings.impact: Invalid checksums could be accepted or valid ones rejected incorrectly.
findings.remediation: Add comprehensive checksum validation test cases.

findings.title: No Data Retention Policy Enforcement
findings.description: CLEANUP_DAYS is defined but not automatically enforced. Users must manually run --cleanup. There's no automated cleanup mechanism.
findings.impact: Old handoff data may accumulate indefinitely, violating data minimization principles.
findings.remediation: Implement automatic cleanup on compaction or add a scheduled task mechanism.

findings.title: No Audit Trail for Handoff Access
findings.description: Handoff data is read and written without logging who accessed it, when, or for what purpose. This makes security auditing difficult.
findings.evidence: No logging of handoff access operations.
findings.impact: Unable to investigate data breaches or unauthorized access.
findings.remediation: Add structured logging for all handoff operations with user, timestamp, and operation type.

findings.title: Missing Privacy Controls
findings.description: Handoff data may contain sensitive information (user requests, file paths, code snippets) but there's no mechanism to redact or encrypt sensitive fields.
findings.evidence: handoff_data stores raw user input without sanitization.
findings.impact: Sensitive data could be exposed in logs or backups.
findings.remediation: Implement sensitive data detection and redaction, or encryption at rest.

findings.title: Silent Failures in Schema Validation
findings.description: Schema validation failures return 0 silently without informing the user. This means corrupted handoffs are skipped without any indication.
findings.impact: Users don't know when handoff restoration fails, leading to confusion.
findings.remediation: Log schema validation failures at WARNING level and output user-visible message.

findings.title: Inadequate Error Messages
findings.description: Validation error messages don't provide enough context for users to fix the issue (e.g., which value failed validation, what the valid range is).
findings.evidence: raise ValueError(f"progress_percent must be between 0 and 100, got {progress_percent}")
findings.impact: Users struggle to debug validation failures.
findings.remediation: Include field name, current value, valid range, and suggestion in error messages.

findings.title: No Validation of Handoff Data Completeness
findings.description: build_handoff_data() doesn't validate that required fields have meaningful values. Empty strings or None for important fields are accepted.
findings.evidence: No validation of next_steps content beyond type checking.
findings.impact: Handoffs with missing critical data get saved, causing confusion on restore.
findings.remediation: Add validation rules for data quality (e.g., non-empty task_name, sensible progress values).

findings.title: Root Cause: File Locking Implementation Flaw
findings.description: The file locking mechanism attempts to implement cross-platform locking using O_CREAT|O_EXCL, which has race conditions on network filesystems and doesn't actually prevent concurrent reads. This is the root cause of potential task tracker corruption.
findings.evidence: Lock file creation with retry loop but no actual locking of target file
findings.impact: Data corruption under concurrent compaction, especially on network drives or with rapid successive compactions.
findings.remediation: Use platform-specific mandatory locking (fcntl.lockf on Unix, Windows file locking APIs) or switch to SQLite with proper transaction isolation.

findings.title: Root Cause: Checksum Algorithm Choice
findings.description: SHA256 is used for checksums, which is cryptographically secure but unnecessarily slow for data integrity checking. The root cause is using a cryptographic hash when a non-cryptographic one (xxHash, MurmurHash) would be sufficient and 10-100x faster.
findings.impact: Compaction takes longer than necessary, especially for large handoffs with frequent checksums.
findings.remediation: Use xxhash or similar fast non-cryptographic hash for integrity checking.

findings.title: Root Cause: Transcript Parsing Performance
findings.description: Transcript parsing loads entire JSON files into memory. The root cause is not using streaming JSON parser for large transcripts.
findings.impact: High memory usage for large transcripts, potential OOM on memory-constrained systems.
findings.remediation: Use ijson or similar streaming JSON parser for large files.
